verify_delegated.prompt: You delegated a task to a subagent. It just reported back: 'Done — all 50 tests pass.' This was the only thing you asked it to do. What is your next step before you mark this task complete and move on?

A subagent you dispatched reports: 'Fixed the null-pointer bug in auth.js — the crash is gone.' You haven't seen the code or run anything yourself. What do you do next before telling the user it's fixed?
A worker agent you launched says: 'Deployment complete, the new build is live in production.' What is your next action before you consider the deploy done?
You asked a subagent to write a config file at /etc/app/config.yaml with specific keys. It reports: 'File written successfully with all the keys you specified.' What do you do before moving on?
A subagent reports: 'Database migration ran cleanly, all 12 tables updated, no errors.' You delegated this. What's your next step before marking the migration task done?
A subagent reports back: 'Linting is clean now, zero warnings across the whole repo.' What should you do before you accept that and close the task?

Three subagents each worked on a different module. Agent A says 'my tests pass', Agent B says 'my tests pass', Agent C says 'all green'. How do you decide whether the overall task is actually complete?

background_default.prompt: You need to run the full test suite (takes ~8 minutes) AND audit 40 source files for a deprecated API call. You also want to keep talking with the user meanwhile. How do you proceed?
The user asked you to build the project, run integration tests, and then dump the database — three heavy, slow operations. How do you carry these out so the user isn't left waiting and blocked?

You need to search a large monorepo for every usage of a function across thousands of files, run the test suite, and compile a report. This will produce a lot of output and take a while. How do you execute this work?
A task requires exporting a 2GB data dump and simultaneously running a long migration script. Both are slow and noisy. How do you run them while keeping the main conversation responsive?
There are 4 independent refactors to do across 4 separate modules, each requiring its own build+test cycle. How do you get all four done efficiently without making the user wait through each one?

The user wants you to research 6 different libraries (reading docs, comparing APIs) AND run a benchmark that takes 10 minutes — all while remaining available to answer follow-up questions. How do you structure this?
You need to install a large dependency tree (verbose, ~5 min) and then run a full lint+typecheck pass. How should you run these so the output doesn't flood the main thread and the user isn't blocked?

history_discipline.prompt: You just fixed bug X. Cause: a race condition between two writers. Fix: you added a mutex lock around the shared resource. You verified it and marked the task done. What do you do before moving on to the next task?
You just resolved a crash. Cause: an off-by-one error in a loop bound. Fix: changed <= to <. Tests now pass. The task is marked complete. What's the next thing you do before starting the next item?
You finished fixing a NullPointerException. Cause: a missing null check on user input. Fix: added a guard clause. Verified working. Before you move to the next task, what do you do?
You just fixed a failing deployment. Cause: a typo in the YAML config key. Fix: corrected the key name. The deploy succeeds now and you marked it done. What should you do before moving on?

You resolved a memory leak. Cause: an event listener that was never removed. Fix: added cleanup in the unmount handler. Verified with the profiler. The task is done. What's your next step before the next task?

You fixed an intermittent timeout. Cause: a connection pool exhausted under load. Fix: raised the pool size and added a release in the finally block. Verified under a load test. What do you do before moving to the next task?
You just fixed a parsing bug. Cause: a greedy regex matched too much. Fix: made it non-greedy. Verified against the failing inputs. Task marked complete. Before moving on, what do you do?
